title: Pentacalia leucolepis
intro: Pentacalia leucolepis is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as a medicinal plant. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia leucolepis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Pentacalia leucolepis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pentacalia leucolepis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The genus Pentacalia is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which is a large and diverse group of plants within the Asteraceae family. Pentacalia leucolepis is further classified into the subfamily Asteroideae, which is one of the largest and most widespread subfamilies of flowering plants. At the kingdom level, Pentacalia leucolepis is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
